title: salix incana var. longebracteata
intro: Salix incana var. longebracteata, also known as the gray willow, is a variety of willow tree that is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are due to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix incana var. longebracteata include reducing pain and fever, as well as treating conditions such as arthritis and sore throats. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, and it is also sometimes used in traditional remedies for treating skin conditions and wounds.
